title: dactylis glomerata var. scabra
intro: Dactylis glomerata var. scabra, also known as rough cock's foot or rough orchard grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and rough stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Dactylis glomerata var. scabra include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
